name: Red Snapper Courtbouillon
recipeIngredient: 6 lb Red snapper, fillet or whole
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat enough shortening in a large pot to cover the bottom of the pot. Get it hot.
2. Add celery, shallots, onions, bell peppers, parsley, and lemons. Cook slowly covered until the vegetables are soft and the onions are transparent.
3. Remove from the pot and set aside.
4. In the same pot, heat margarine and add flour gradually, stirring to brown the roux.
5. Add the cooked vegetable mix back into the pot. Cook on low for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Add mushrooms, tomatoes, tap water, bay leaves, and Worcestershire sauce. Cook on low for 1-1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
7. Add fish fillets, either whole or in pieces, and cook for another hour over low heat.
8. Add oysters 20 minutes before serving over hot rice. This recipe can be used with any type of fish.
